source: Claims against Malayan Government. Mr. J. Pascheove__CO273:670:8
filename: Claims against Malayan Government. Mr. J. Pascheove__CO273:670:8.txt
summary:
- File concerns a **claim for unpaid rent against the Straits Settlements (Malayan) Government** arising from a **house lease in Singapore** during/after the **Japanese occupation** (documents dated **1942–1944**, mainly 1942–43).  
- **Claimant:** **Mr. I. (J.) Pascheove** (associated with **Paskoe’s Ltd.**, resident in **Australia/Melbourne**); represented by **Messrs. Arthur Robinson & Co., solicitors**.  
- **Property/terms alleged:** house in **Clarke Road, Singapore**, leased to the **Straits Settlements Government** for **3 years from 1 Jan 1941** at **$200 per month**; rent said to have been paid **up to Jan 1942**; house occupied by **Mr. Middlebrook (Immigration & Passport Officer)**; lease said to be signed by **Mr. Jones (Colonial Secretary)**.  
- Internal legal view (Colonial Office advisers incl. **Mr. Duncan**, with reference to English case law and **Paradine v Jane**) was that, **for a fixed-term lease**, rent would **likely remain legally payable for the whole term despite force majeure/Japanese occupation**, unless a **termination right** existed and was exercised (assumptions noted about applicable law and lease terms).  
- Administrative complication: officials initially said they **could not verify the lease** until reoccupation/access to records; later it emerged a **copy of the lease was in a sealed packet sent from Singapore to Canberra** (Packet **No. 1120**), held by the **UK High Commissioner in Canberra**.  
- Policy debate: Arthur Robinson & Co. urged **early compromise settlements** because records might be destroyed and refugees lacked funds; Colonial Office discussion weighed **equity vs precedent** and concern about **not prejudicing future Military Administration handling** of similar claims on reoccupation.  
- **Decision/communications:** despite legal arguments suggesting liability, the official line communicated (per **Despatch No. 140, 2 Dec 1942**, and later **Draft No. 244, 16 Nov 1943**) was that **no action/payment would be taken at present**, other than **registering the claim**, citing inability to act until reoccupation and that payment was due **locally/in local currency**; the Secretary of State would **not object to releasing the sealed packet** to the owners but stated he **would not revise the decision** even if the lease details were confirmed.
